title: rosa sempervirens var. scandens
intro: Rosa sempervirens var. scandens, also known as the climbing rose or creeping rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Rosa sempervirens var. scandens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant effects, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Rosa sempervirens var. scandens can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
